entity_relationship_types.description: Ontological subclass relationship (child is a type of parent)
Entity is a component of the target (e.g., gene in a pathway)
Gene encodes a protein or other gene product
Physical or functional interaction between two entities
Entity regulates activity, expression, or function of the target
Statistical or curated association between two entities
Entities co-occur in literature without directed relationship
Disease has causal basis in gene mutation (gain/loss-of-function or somatic)
Disease has associated phenotypic feature (HP term or characteristic)
Disease has anatomical location or cellular origin (UBERON/CL)
Disease has basis in disruption of a biological process or function (GO term)
Disease responds to or has basis in accumulation of a chemical (ChEBI)
